texto: Canoes, too, are unobtrusive; they don't storm the natural world or ride over it, but drift in upon it as a part of its own silence. As you either care about what the land is or not, so do you like or dislike quiet things--sailboats, or rainy green mornings in foreign places, or a grazing herd, or the ruins of old monasteries in the mountains. . . . Chances for being quiet nowadays are limited.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a John Graves: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Canoes, too, are unobtrusive; they don't storm the natural world or ride over it, but drift in up…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, canoe, nature pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e bom-dia.
